evidence: we expect to construct, through GenCo, 400 MW of new battery storage and a new power generation facility consisting of two 1,300 MW CCGTs, which are expected to reach commercial operation between 2028 and 2032
we expect to incur significant additional long-term debt and issue additional equity in NiSource...to support construction of the Contract Assets
the generating capacity of the Contract Assets, when fully delivered into service, is expected to be approximately equivalent to the generating capacity of all NIPSCO s existing generating assets
we entered into the ADS Contract, a customized agreement under which NIPSCO will provide electric service to ADS by procuring power from GenCo, which will develop related generation assets
accurately predicting future power needs of data centers due to rapidly changing technology and market dynamics
Deployment or adoption of new or emerging business technologies, including artificial intelligence, Internet of Things (IoT) devices, and cloud-based platforms...heightens our exposure to risks

a change in investor sentiment toward us or the utilities industry or the cloud-computing, artificial intelligence and data center industry generally could adversely affect our ability to raise the necessary capital

DATA CENTER OPERATIONS AND STRATEGY RISKS Data center growth...provide significant financial, operational, and regulatory risks...Construction delays, cost overruns or performance issues with the Contract Assets could reduce our returns
Our partnership with ADS exposes us to significant customer concentration risk...ADS has the right to terminate the ADS Contract for convenience following certain notice periods

capex_mentions.quote: 400 MW of new battery storage and a new power generation facility consisting of two 1,300 MW CCGTs, which are expected to reach commercial operation between 2028 and 2032
capex_mentions.context: Contract Assets to be constructed by GenCo under the ADS Contract for data center power supply

capex_mentions.quote: significant additional long-term debt and issue additional equity in NiSource...to support construction of the Contract Assets
capex_mentions.context: Financing required for data center generation and transmission infrastructure build-out

limitations: NiSource is primarily a utility company providing power infrastructure to data center customers (notably ADS, an Amazon subsidiary); AI investment intensity score reflects large-scale power infrastructure capex tied to data center demand rather than direct AI software/hardware spend by NiSource itself.

Dollar amounts for total capex are not explicitly quoted in the excerpts; capacity figures (400 MW storage, 2x1,300 MW CCGTs) are used as proxies for scale — score of 4 reflects quantified capacity commitments without a single total dollar figure.

AI narrative concreteness scored 3 because NiSource's AI exposure is indirect (serving AI data center customers); the company names the ADS Contract and infrastructure initiatives but does not describe its own internal AI use cases in deployment.

Risk disclosure depth scored 4 because the excerpts contain an extensive dedicated 'DATA CENTER OPERATIONS AND STRATEGY RISKS' section with named, company-specific risk mechanisms (customer concentration, MISO accreditation, construction overruns, ASC 980 accounting risk, financing risk), though no quantified risk dollar amounts or named internal AI governance bodies are present.
